spec_paraphrase: D-Rank cited as Stage 3 rank-budget paper
paper_verbatim: D-Rank, a framework with layer-wise balanced Dynamic Rank allocation for LLMs compression
notes: Paper title/role acknowledged in spec §6 and §13 catalog.

paper_verbatim: more than 15 lower perplexity on the C4 dataset with LLaMA-3-8B at 20% compression ratio
notes: Reference numbers — informational; not a spec invariant.

spec_paraphrase: Spec groups across n_experts (MoE) per (layer, matrix_type), not across layers per shared-basis group
paper_verbatim: we can first concatenate matrices within the same group horizontally
notes: D-Rank's grouping concept (n layers per group) is repurposed as n_experts per (layer, matrix_type). Sanctioned by D7 (paper-internal repurposing).

spec_paraphrase: Spec line 429: 'Eigendecomposition replaces Cholesky (handles rank-deficient B natively)' — but this is for AA-SVD whitening of B, not for D-Rank's S=cholesky(X⊤X). Spec does NOT describe a whitened S_g·W_g matrix being formed before Reff computation in Phase B at all.
paper_verbatim: SS⊤ = cholesky(X⊤X) and W = S^{-1}(SW). ... we apply SVD to the scaled matrix SW instead of W
notes: FINDING: D-Rank's effective rank in paper is computed from singular values of the WHITENED matrix S·W (Eq. 1 uses λ^i_g = σ_i(S_g W_g)²). Spec Phase B (line 366) shows p_i = σ_i²/Σσ_j² without specifying which matrix's singular values — appears to use raw weight singular values, not whitened. Stage 3 Phase A collects B = X_post^T X_post (post-prune student) which differs from paper's X^T X on the original calibration. No mention of FP64 for the matrix used. This is a numerical deviation not covered by §12.

paper_verbatim: Notably, when n = 1 only, the procedure is the standard SVD-LLM approach.
notes: MoE adaptation has no per-layer n; n is mapped to n_experts. Not directly applicable.

spec_paraphrase: p_i = σ_i² / Σ_j σ_j² (no qualification of which matrix)
paper_verbatim: p^i_g = λ^i_g / Σ_j λ^j_g where λ = (σ of S_g W_g)²
notes: Spec Eq.2-style p_i normalization matches form, but does not state the singular values must come from the whitened matrix S·W. Tied to the whitening omission above.

paper_verbatim: Reff(g) = exp(- Σ_i p_i log p_i)
notes: Effective-rank formula matches verbatim.

spec_paraphrase: high-entropy matrices get more rank, low-entropy matrices get less
paper_verbatim: higher effective rank suggests higher information density of the group
V matrices have far higher Reff than K/Q (Table 1)
notes: Empirical evidence; spec is for MoE (no Q,K,V in Stage 3).
Empirical observation; not a required spec invariant.
Sanctioned by D7. Paper-internal accounting (param-cost-per-rank) is preserved in spirit but applied to MoE expert dimension.

spec_paraphrase: T_budget is the global rank budget derived from svd_rank_ratio
paper_verbatim: Tbudget = T(1 − θ) = Σ k_g ω — total number of PARAMETERS in the compressed module
notes: FINDING: Spec calls T_budget a 'rank budget' but uses it in a formula whose derivation requires it to be a parameter budget. This is internally inconsistent and may produce a different optimum than paper Eq.7. Either the variable name or the formula is wrong.

paper_verbatim: minimize Σ Reff(g)/k_g s.t. Σ k_g ω = Tbudget
notes: Optimization setup not stated explicitly in spec; closed form (Eq.7) is the only thing cited.
Intermediate derivation; not required in spec.

spec_paraphrase: Eq.6 gives proportionality only (acknowledged in spec line 368)
notes: Spec correctly notes Eq.6 is proportionality only; final form is from Eq.7.

paper_verbatim: k_g = ( Tbudget / Σ_{j} sqrt(Reff(j) · ω) ) · ( sqrt(Reff(g)) / sqrt(ω) )
notes: FINDING: Denominator differs structurally. Paper: Σ √(R·ω) (with multiplication). Spec: Σ √(R/ω) (with division). These coincide only when ω is constant across all groups g'. With per-projection ω variation (different gate/up/down d_in vs d_out — gate/up are 2048×512, down is 512×2048 — d_in+d_out happens to equal but gate vs down may have asymmetric ω if projection bias is also baked in), the two formulas give different allocations. Spec's formula additionally implies budget constraint Σk = T_b (rank budget) which contradicts paper's Σk·ω = T_b (param budget). Recommend spec correct denominator to Σ√(R·ω) OR explicitly redefine T_budget as a rank budget AND change numerator to √R (without ω) to be self-consistent.

spec_paraphrase: Spec Phase B computes p_i, R_eff, k_g but never describes the prerequisite of forming S_g·W_g (whitened matrix) first.
paper_verbatim: First, for each group, weight matrices across n layers are concatenated horizontally and multiplied by S to form scaled matrix SgWg ... then we calculate the effective rank
notes: FINDING: The pipeline pre-condition (multiply by whitening factor S before computing Reff) is omitted from spec. This compounds with the eq1/whitening findings — without S, Reff is being computed on raw weight singular values, which is the SVD-LLM-without-activation-aware step the paper explicitly improves on.

paper_verbatim: WV consistently exhibits much larger Reff than WQ, WK
notes: Q,K,V not in Stage 3 scope (MoE expert MLPs only).

paper_verbatim: Lagrangian alone is unfair to WV
notes: Same — Q,K,V out of scope.
Out of scope (no attention compression in Stage 3).

Spec instead applies a per-projection bias gate=1.33 / up=0.67 / down=1.0 (D7a). This is a DIFFERENT redistribution mechanism, not the paper's Q,K→V β rebalance. D7a explicitly cites NOT having a per-projection multiplier in the paper, so the deviation is sanctioned. Note: the paper's β=0.3-0.4 sweep is therefore not applicable; D7a uses fixed multipliers.
Out of scope (no K matrix in Stage 3).
Out of scope.
Out of scope.
β not used (no attention rebalance in MoE Stage 3).

paper_verbatim: GQA dimensions inflate effective rank with horizontal concat
notes: Target Qwen3 model is MoE — concatenation is across experts, not GQA.
Not applicable to MoE expert grouping.

paper_verbatim: Basis Sharing degradation as n grows on LLaMA-3-8B

notes: Spec evaluates with WikiText-2 PPL + lm-eval-harness similarly; not strict adherence required for D-Rank claim.
Spec target is Qwen3-30B MoE — different model class, novel application.

spec_paraphrase: Eigendecomposition with dtype-aware noise floor (bf16→1e-2, fp16→1e-3, fp32→1e-6) — no FP64 path
paper_verbatim: We use FP64 to maintain the computational precision of matrix S.
notes: FINDING: Paper explicitly mandates FP64 precision for the whitening matrix S. Spec uses fp32 max for B-cov eigendecomposition (line 430). On H200, FP64 is supported — there is no hardware reason to deviate. Combined with the missing whitening step (no S formed at all), this is a compounded numerical-fidelity gap. Not covered by Ch.12.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 3 (B-cov) uses 512 calibration samples × seq_len 2048 (line 90), source dataset not specified for D-Rank specifically
paper_verbatim: Matrix S is derived from 256 samples of WikiText-2 with a sequence length of 2048.
notes: FINDING: Paper specifies 256 × 2048; spec Stage 3 uses 512 × 2048. Doubling is fine in principle (more calib = better cov estimate), but no S whitening is computed in any case — the calibration goes into B = X_post^T X_post for AA-SVD, not into S = chol(X^T X) for D-Rank. The 256 calibration count for D-Rank is not separately maintained.

paper_verbatim: downstream layer adaptive update at ratio≥40%
notes: Spec target compression is 30% (svd_rank_ratio derived from target_total_reduction=0.30); below the 40% threshold where the downstream-update kicks in. Not a required behavior at current target; would become a deviation only if spec moves above 40%.

spec_paraphrase: Spec groups all of {gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj} in MoE Stage 3 (i.e., down_proj IS factored alongside gate/up).
paper_verbatim: matrices like WQ, WK, WV, Wup, and Wgate ... are grouped and compressed in our experiments when n > 1, while Wdown and WO are not grouped.
notes: Paper's rule applies to n>1 cross-LAYER grouping. Spec's grouping is across EXPERTS (single layer), so the rule technically does not apply. D7 sanctions the n→n_experts repurposing. down_proj IS compressed in spec — paper would not group {down,O} but spec applies a per-(layer,matrix_type) rank without horizontal concat across experts (each expert factored independently within the rank budget). Effectively no horizontal-concat is happening at all in spec — just per-expert SVD with shared k_g. This sidesteps the paper's concern.
β not used; D7a uses fixed gate/up/down multipliers instead.

paper_verbatim: β = 0.3-0.4 consistently yields the lowest PPL
notes: β rebalance not implemented (no attention compression); D7a substitutes a non-paper per-projection multiplier.
Spec uses Stage 5 KD recovery with different settings; LoRA is a different recovery path.
Confirms the parameter-budget interpretation: Σ k_g ω = T_budget.
Authoritative denominator form: Σ √(Reff·ω). Spec uses Σ √(Reff/ω) — see eq7 finding.

paper_verbatim: k_g = ( Tbudget / Σ_{j=1}^G sqrt(Reff(j) ω) ) · ( sqrt(Reff(g)) / sqrt(ω) )
notes: Same finding as eq7 — appendix Eq.19 is the authoritative restatement and confirms the spec's denominator form differs from the paper's. Recorded once in findings via eq7.

paper_verbatim: higher Reff → more rank, higher ω → less rank, fixed Tbudget

spec_paraphrase: ω = n_experts × (d_out + d_in) — n=n_experts, not n=layers per group
paper_verbatim: ω = d1 + n d2; n=1 → ω = d1 + d2
notes: D7 sanctions repurposing n to mean expert-count instead of layer-count.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not horizontally concatenate experts; each expert is factored independently with shared k_g (with per-expert Swift-SVD+ refinement).
paper_verbatim: we can first concatenate matrices within the same group horizontally
notes: Sanctioned implicitly under D7 — MoE adaptation drops the shared-basis step. Could be more explicit in §12.

paper_verbatim: B C^(i) decomposition with shared B
notes: Shared-basis decomposition not used in spec.
Inherited implicitly via Eq.7 use; no separate spec assertion.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses eigendecomposition of B = X_post^T X_post (post-prune student input cov) for AA-SVD, not Cholesky of X^T X for D-Rank whitening. No S matrix is computed for D-Rank.
paper_verbatim: SS⊤ = cholesky(X⊤X) — S is the lower Cholesky factor of X⊤X
notes: FINDING: Two distinct concerns. (1) D-Rank specifies Cholesky factor of X^T X — spec replaces with eigendecomposition (line 429 says 'Eigendecomposition replaces Cholesky'). The eigendecomposition of B is mathematically related to (B)^{1/2} (symmetric square root), which is NOT the same as the Cholesky factor S where SS^T = X^T X. (2) Critically, the spec never forms an S matrix or whitens any weight before computing R_eff. Both are unsanctioned numerical deviations from the paper.
